Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 1. Understand the Goal: The user wants an analysis of an event log based on a provided process description. The analysis needs to identify normative violations, desiderata failures, and suggest interpretations/justifications for these behaviors.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 2. Deconstruct the Process Rules (Normative vs. Desiderata): Normative (Strict) Rules: Sequence: Application Received -> Credit Check -> Risk Assessment -> Loan Decision -> Inform Applicant -> [If Approved: Finalize Documentation] -> Close Case. Ordering Rules: Credit Check must occur before Risk Assessment. Risk Assessment must occur before Loan Decision. Inform Applicant must occur after Loan Decision. If Approved: Finalize Documentation must occur before Close Case. If Rejected: DO NOT finalize documentation before closing. No skipping steps, no reversing order, no contradictory actions. Desiderata (Recommended) Rules: Credit Check within 24 hours of Application Received. Inform Applicant within 48 hours of the loan decision. Perform a Follow-up Call before closing the case.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 3. Analyze the Event Log Case by Case: Case 1: Trace: Application Received (09:00) -> Credit Check (09:30) -> Risk Assessment (10:00) -> Loan Decision Approved (11:00) -> Inform Applicant (13:00) -> Finalize Documentation (13:30) -> Close Case (14:00). Normative Check: Order is correct. Approved -> Finalized Doc -> Close Case. No violations. Desiderata Check: Credit check within 24h? Yes (30 mins). Inform within 48h? Yes (2 hours). Follow-up call? No. Conclusion: No normative violations. Fails desiderata (missing Follow-up Call). Case 2: Trace: Application Received (08:00) -> Risk Assessment (08:20) -> Credit Check (09:00) -> Loan Decision Rejected (09:30) -> Finalize Documentation (10:00) -> Close Case (10:30). Normative Check: Risk Assessment before Credit Check. Violation. Decision Rejected -> Finalize Documentation. Violation. Missing: Inform Applicant. Violation. Desiderata Check: Missing follow-up call, but moot. Conclusion: Severe normative violations (order, logic, skipping). Case 3: Trace: Application Received (Jan 12 09:00) -> Credit Check (Jan 14 10:00) -> Risk Assessment (Jan 14 11:00) -> Loan Decision Approved (Jan 14 12:00) -> Inform Applicant (Jan 16 15:00) -> Finalize Documentation (Jan 17 09:00) -> Close Case (Jan 17 10:00). Normative Check: Sequence correct. No violations. Desiderata Check: Credit check within 24h? No (~49 hours). Inform within 48h? No (~51 hours). Follow-up call? No. Conclusion: No normative violations. Fails all three desiderata.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Part 3: Interpretations/Justifications: Brainstorm realistic business reasons for the observed deviations. Case 2: Why Risk Assessment before Credit Check? Maybe an automated pre-screening based on internal data? Or a severe process failure/training issue. Why Finalize Doc after Rejection? Maybe a system glitch, or they started preparing docs before the final rejection came through, or Finalize here meant closing out the rejected file (terminology confusion). Why no Inform Applicant? Maybe the applicant was informed verbally during the Finalize Documentation step, or a system failure prevented the logging/sending of the notification. Case 3: Why delays? Backlog, weekend processing, waiting for applicant to provide additional info. Why no Follow-up call? High workload, forgotten, or deemed unnecessary since the loan was approved and docs were signed.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: 4. Structure the Response: Part 1: Normative Violations: Go through each case, state if it violates normative rules, and explain exactly which rules were broken based on the deconstructed list. Part 2: Desiderata Failures: Go through each case, state if it fails to meet recommended practices, and list the specific failures. Emphasize that these are suboptimal but not severe. Part 3: Interpretations/Justifications: Brainstorm realistic business reasons for the observed deviations.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 5. Draft the Content (Iterative refinement for clarity and tone): Self-Correction during drafting: Make sure to explicitly state that Case 1 has no normative violations. Clearly separate normative from desiderata. Ensure the tone reflects the severity difference (strict vs. recommended). 6. Final Polish: Check against all user prompts. Did I identify normative violations and explain? Yes. Did I identify desiderata failures and clarify they arent severe? Yes. Did I suggest interpretations? Yes.
